channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: House GOP elections chair reveals which voter blocs Republicans are targeting ahead of 2026
item.description: NRCC Chairman Richard Hudson credited President Donald Trump for Republicans' electoral success and predicted it will continue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk's DOGE commission gains access to sensitive Treasury payment systems: AP sources
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Department of Government Efficiency, run by President Donald Trump's billionaire adviser and Tesla CEO Elon Musk, has gained access to sensitive Treasury data including Social Security and Medicare customer payment systems, according to two people familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats accused of corruption look to Trump for clemency
item.description: Democrats accused of corruption are seeking relief from President Trump as he hands out scores of pardons and drops charges against his political allies amid his crusade against the justice system that made him a convicted felon.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US military conducts airstrikes against Islamic State operatives in Somalia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has conducted airstrikes against Islamic State operatives in Somalia, the first attacks in the African nation during President Donald Trump’s second term. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Saturday that the strikes by U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, AAMER MADHANI and OMAR FARUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump fires the director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: President Donald Trump has fired the director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, Rohit Chopra, in the latest purge of a Biden administration holdover.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Where Trump's Cabinet nominees stand in Senate confirmation process
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr., Tulsi Gabbard and Kash Patel testify before Senate committees on Capitol Hill on Thursday as urgency builds to confirm President Donald Trump’s Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As Trump vows to embrace fossil fuels, U.S. climate policy won't change quickly
item.description: President Trump's first weeks in office included executive orders that aim to stop or roll back former President Biden's ambitious climate agenda and redirect the country's attention to fossil fuels.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady, MPR News

item.title: Democrats elect Minnesota's Ken Martin as DNC chair on first ballot
item.description: Ken Martin of Minnesota is the new chair of the Democratic National Committee. Members of the DNC elected Martin on the first ballot in a vote outside of Washington D.C.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: US aid agency is in upheaval during foreign assistance freeze and staff departures
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Trump administration changes have upended the U.S. agency charged with providing humanitarian aid to countries overseas, with dozens of senior officials put on leave, thousands of contractors laid off, and a sweeping freeze imposed on billions of dollars in foreign assistance.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Second federal judge orders temporary pause to Trump administration efforts to freeze funding
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A second federal judge on Friday ordered a temporary pause in Trump administration efforts to freeze federal funding in the latest twist over the spending of trillions of dollars in grants and loans.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How non-violent undocumented immigrants could be swept up in raids
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – In two weeks of the second Trump administration, the White House is staying true to tough immigration policies with ICE raids across the country. Several have happened in the tri-state area.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: CBS agrees to hand over '60 Minutes' Harris interview transcripts to FCC
item.description: CBS says it will turn over unedited transcripts of its October interview with Kamala Harris to the Federal Communications Commission.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Air traffic controllers were initially offered buyouts and told to consider leaving government
item.description: Just a day before a deadly midair collision at Reagan National Airport outside Washington, D.C., employees at the Federal Aviation Administration were sent an offer to resign with eight months' pay.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he and Putin could do something 'significant' toward ending Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said his administration has already had “very serious” discussions with Russia about its war in Ukraine and that he and Russian President Vladimir Putin could soon take “significant” action toward ending the grinding conflict.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration to pause federal government websites in effort to eliminate DEI, anti-Trump content
item.description: The Trump administration will put a pause on most federal government websites Friday evening in an effort to eliminate DEI content and any language that is opposed to President Trump’s agenda, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congested Reagan National site of several previous close calls
item.description: The complex airspace around Washington, D.C., has resulted in at least three near misses in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: New York expands efforts to stop human trafficking
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York is expanding its efforts to stop human trafficking. Gov. Kathy Hochul recently signed a series of bills to shed light on the issue, which targets young women. The NYPD's human trafficking task force conducted more than 300 operations in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: More migrants deported under Biden, Baja state official says
item.description: Tijuana Mayor Ismael Burgueño Ruiz says he will not lift an emergency declaration he made last month as a way to prepare for mass deportations from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs Laken Riley Act, plans to send illegal immigrants to Guantanamo
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's directing the opening of a detention center at Guantanamo Bay to hold up to 30,000 migrants who are living without legal permission in the United States. The U.S. military base has been used to house detainees from the U.S. war on terrorism.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota ACLU: Local jurisdictions have the right to not cooperate with immigration enforcement
item.description: During President Donald Trump’s first week in office, his new Department of Justice announced plans to potentially criminally prosecute local and state officials who fail to “comply with lawful immigration-related commands and requests.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Ngoc Bui, MPR News

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Medicaid, SNAP, other benefits safe for Nevadans, Lombardo says
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo issued a statement Tuesday to clarify what is and is not affected by executive orders issued by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration to offer buyouts to all federal workers ahead of return to office
item.description: The Trump administration is offering all 2 million federal employees what amounts to a buyout if they do not intend to return to work in person later this year, sources confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump orders federal funding freeze: What it means for NYers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – President Donald Trump’s administration has issued a pause on all federal grants and loans, according to a memo from Matthew Vaeth, acting director of the Office of Management and Budget. The memo was sent on Monday but the freeze goes into effect Tuesday at 5 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Florida House and Senate leaders take charge in special session showdown
item.description: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis pushed for a special session to help boost President Donald Trump's immigration efforts. But that session was blocked by Senate and House leadership Monday.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5
